title: Query Understanding, Filters, Rewriting, Expansion, and Decomposition · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved trace contains a plausible bounded query-plan compiler with one ingestion, normalization, chunking, lexical, embedding, index, filter, fusion, reranking, context, generation, citation, authorization, latency, or recovery defect; identify the first failed stage before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded query-plan compiler and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Extract intent, entities, exact terms, time, language, tenant, source, and authorization filters through a validated query schema.
Preserve original query, rewrite purpose, version, generated alternatives, filters, and per-rewrite retrieval contribution.
Evaluate synonyms, acronyms, spelling, pseudo-relevance feedback, and domain vocabulary without drifting away from user intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Rag Decomposition Clarification Budget
steps.instruction:

content.text: A debug RAG engineering team handles case 8604 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to classify intent, validate filters, bound rewrites, preserve lineage, and ask when ambiguity remains; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Decompose bounded multi-part or multi-hop questions, preserve dependencies, and ask for clarification when ambiguity exceeds policy.

steps.title: RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Decomposition Clarification Budget
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-decomposition-clarification-budget-1e6lg3" that uses case 3859 to classify intent, validate filters, bound rewrites, preserve lineage, and ask when ambiguity remains. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Decomposition Clarification Budget.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Decomposition Clarification Budget into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Rag Decomposition Clarification Budget, keep Rag Decomposition Clarification Budget connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-decomposition-clarification-budget-1e6lg3
# Competency: rag-decomposition-clarification-budget.
# Case 3859: a regulated document deletion request.
# Operating constraint: retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity.
# Changed case: change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence.
def evidence_rag_decomposition_clarification_budget_1e6lg3(query: str = "reset MFA for contractor", intent: str = "procedure", filters_valid: bool = True, ambiguity: int = 1, rewrite_count: int = 2, original_preserved: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1e6lg3 = "1e6lg3-3gl6e1-3859"
    assert evidence_variant_1e6lg3
    evidence_axes_1e6lg3 = (
        "corpus1e6lg3",
        "query3gl6e1",
        "tenant3859",
        "index1e6lg33859",
        "prompt3gl6e13859",
        "evaluation38591e6lg3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1e6lg3)) == 6
    if not query.strip() or not intent:
        return False, 0, "query-intent"
    if not filters_valid:
        return False, rewrite_count, "filter-schema"
    if ambiguity < 0 or rewrite_count < 0 or rewrite_count > 5:
        return False, rewrite_count, "rewrite-budget"
    if not original_preserved:
        return False, rewrite_count, "query-lineage"
    if ambiguity > 2:
        return False, rewrite_count, "clarification-required"
    assert rewrite_count <= 5
    return True, rewrite_count, "bounded-query-plan"
